ddls:
CREATE TABLE "energy_demand" (
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the energy demand data. */,
  "model_geography" TEXT /* Main geographical area where the data is collected. */,
  "sub_geography1" TEXT /* First level sub-geography within the model geography. */,
  "sub_geography2" TEXT /* Second level sub-geography within the model geography. */,
  "consumer_type1" TEXT /* Primary type of consumer (e.g., RURAL, URBAN). */,
  "consumer_type2" TEXT /* Secondary type of consumer (e.g., Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4). */,
  "energy_demand" REAL /* Energy demand in the specified unit (e.g., MW). */,
  "quarter" TEXT /* Quarter of the year (e.g., Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4). */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., internal, external). */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the data was last updated. */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the data. */,
  "forecast_accuracy" REAL /* Measure of how accurate the forecast was compared to actual demand (e.g., percentage error). */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Information about weather conditions during the period (e.g., temperature, precipitation). */,
  "holiday_indicator" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the period includes a holiday. */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the area. */,
  "infrastructure_quality" INTEGER /* Quality of the energy infrastructure in the area. */,
  "economic_activity" REAL /* Measure of economic activity in the area. */,
  "geography_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the geography table. */,
  "consumer_type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the consumer_types table. */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the data_sources table. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year", "model_geography", "sub_geography1", "sub_geography2", "consumer_type1", "consumer_type2")
)

CREATE TABLE "geography" (
  "geography_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the geography. */,
  "model_geography" TEXT /* Main geographical area. */,
  "sub_geography1" TEXT /* First level sub-geography within the model geography. */,
  "sub_geography2" TEXT /* Second level sub-geography within the model geography. */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the geographic center. */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the geographic center. */,
  "area_size" REAL /* Area size in square kilometers. */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the area. */,
  "climate_zone" TEXT /* Climate zone of the area. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("geography_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "consumer_types" (
  "consumer_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the consumer type. */,
  "consumer_type1" TEXT /* Primary type of consumer. */,
  "consumer_type2" TEXT /* Secondary type of consumer. */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the consumer type. */,
  "average_household_size" REAL /* Average household size for the consumer type. */,
  "average_income" REAL /* Average income for the consumer type. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("consumer_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the data source. */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Name of the data source. */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., internal, external, survey). */,
  "reliability_score" INTEGER /* Reliability score of the data source (1-10). */,
  "last_audit_date" TEXT /* Date of the last audit of the data source. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user. */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user. */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user. */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, viewer). */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user. */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login. */,
  "active_status" TEXT /* Active status of the user (e.g., active, inactive). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the role. */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role. */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, delete). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
